narrative: All three watchlist coins extended last week's recovery: bitcoin settled at $62,977 (+0.88%), ethereum outpaced it at +3.00%, and solana added 1.58%, even as Iran-Israel exchange of strikes briefly pulled BTC back from intraday highs of $63,700 per CoinDesk. The rebound faces diverging institutional signals — NYDIG's Greg Cipolaro cited overlapping headwinds (AI rotation, tech IPOs, quantum fears, Strategy sale anxiety) with no single root cause, while ETF outflows at the $60,000 level stand in sharp contrast to February's institutional dip-buying per CoinDesk. On the structural side, Goldman Sachs launched a tokenized real estate fund with Apex and Archax, Securitize cleared NYSE listing approval, and Abra's CEO declared tokenized yield products the next major crypto driver.
worth_watching: Strategy (MSTR) — Michael Saylor hinted at 'adding more dots' on his BTC purchase chart as scrutiny grows; a fresh buy at these levels would be a major sentiment signal per CoinDesk

Meta USDC creator payments — Meta paying creators in stablecoin validates mainstream disbursement use, but the fiat off-ramp gap remains unresolved per CoinDesk; could pressure payment rails to catch up
Monero (XMR) — after AI uncovered Zcash's 4-year Orchard bug, researcher Taylor Hornby added Monero to his audit queue; privacy coin security risk is actively broadening per CoinDesk
